article_title: Tel Aviv University Demonstrates Nanoscale Graphene Switch That Flips Stacking Order With Less Than One Femtojoule of Energy

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Tel Aviv University Demonstrates Nanoscale Graphene Switch That Flips Stacking Order With Less Than One Femtojoule of Energy
content_preview.summary: Researchers achieved reversible switching between graphene polytypes at 30-nanometer scale using sub-nanonewton forces and sub-femtojoule energy, opening a path to slidetronic memory and neuromorphic computing.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team at Tel Aviv University has demonstrated a method for switching the internal stacking arrangement of graphene layers at the nanometer scale, using energy inputs orders of magnitude lower than those required by existing memory technologies. The work, [published in Nature Nanotechnology](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41565-025-02121-w), achieves fully reversible structural transformations in graphene islands as small as 30 nanometers in diameter, consuming less than one femto...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent materials science article with sophisticated technical content explained accessibly. Strong structure covering the what, how, and why with appropriate caveats.
editor_notes.source_verification: Phys.org returned 403 on direct fetch but was archived by the automated system. Nature Nanotechnology returned redirect. ArXiv preprint (2501.06817) confirms core claims: SLAP device, Nirmal Roy and Ben-Shalom authors, polytype switching between Bernal and rhombohedral, NIMS Japan collaboration. Minor discrepancy: article says 'sub-nanonewton' forces but arXiv preprint reports 6 nN — the published Nature Nanotechnology version may have improved results.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core scientific claims verified via arXiv: SLAP architecture, 4-layer graphene with misaligned spacer, circular cavities, polytype switching, slidetronic applications. The 30nm scale and sub-femtojoule energy claims are consistent with the published article scope.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, measured, and scientifically precise. Appropriately skeptical about commercialization path.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of slidetronics or graphene polytype switching in the archive.
concerns: Force level discrepancy between article (sub-nanonewton) and arXiv preprint (6 nN) — may reflect improvements in final published version
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality nanotechnology article with strong scientific content. Approved.
